quotes.text: grug no mass mass like closure. closure make code look mass smart but grug mass mass debug closure and mass mass not fun.
quotes.author: The Grug Brained Developer

quotes.text: Debugging is twice as hard as writing the code in the first place. Therefore, if you write the code as cleverly as possible, you are, by definition, not smart enough to debug it.
quotes.author: Brian Kernighan

quotes.text: The hardest bugs are the ones where your mental model is wrong. You don't see the bug because you're looking at the world wrong.
quotes.author: Bryan Cantrill

quotes.text: 99 little bugs in the code, 99 little bugs. Take one down, patch it around, 127 little bugs in the code.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: 6 months of debugging can save you 5 minutes of reading the documentation.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Given enough eyeballs, all bugs are shallow.
quotes.author: Eric S. Raymond

quotes.text: My development process: 1) Google the error 2) Find a Stack Overflow answer from 2014 3) It works 4) I have no idea why
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Deleted code is debugged code.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: It's not DNS. There's no way it's DNS. It was DNS.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Debugging is like being the detective in a crime movie where you are also the murderer.
quotes.author: Filipe Fortes

quotes.text: The most effective debugging tool is still careful thought, coupled with judiciously placed print statements. Or yelling at a rubber duck.
quotes.author: Unknown (extended from Kernighan)

quotes.text: Sometimes the best debugging technique is to go to sleep and realize the answer at 3am while technically unconscious.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: If debugging is the process of removing bugs, then programming must be the process of putting them in.
quotes.author: Edsger W. Dijkstra

quotes.text: Programming is 10% writing code and 90% understanding why it doesn't work.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Debugging is like being a detective in a crime movie where you're also the suspect.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every programmer has a story about the time they spent 8 hours debugging a missing semicolon. If they say they don't, they're lying.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you're debugging for more than an hour, stop. Walk away. The answer is usually something obvious you're too tired to see.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Event-driven architecture is great until you have to debug why an event from 3 days ago triggered a cascade of failures.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best debugging tool is a good night's sleep. Most bugs look obvious in the morning.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you've been debugging for more than an hour, stop. Walk away. The answer is usually something obvious.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to find a bug is to write a test that reproduces it. Not to stare at the code until it confesses.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Debugging is detective work. You look for clues, form theories, and test them. Sometimes the culprit is you.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best engineers are the ones who can debug without looking at the code. They understand the system, not just the syntax.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best debugging strategy is to add logging, not to add breakpoints. Logging scales. Breakpoints don't.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your stack trace is your best friend. Read it. Understand it. It's trying to help you.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to fix a bug is to write a test that reproduces it, then fix the code until the test passes.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Have you tried turning it off and on again?
quotes.author: Roy (IT Crowd)
